+name=$(basename "$0")

+# TODO: fix this one to become spaces tollerant etc

discussion_comments.comment_body:
todo(blocking): I'm fairly certain quoting `"$args"` will break this script.

Do you think it makes sense to change it to something like:

```diff
diff --git a/run b/run
index f01302c43..ecb392e58 100755
--- a/run
+++ b/run
@@ -10,18 +10,12 @@ red()           { ansi 31 "$@"; }
 
 name=$(basename "$0")
 command=$1
-# TODO: fix this one to become spaces tollerant etc
-#       If to be used as an array, make it
-# args=( "${@:2}" )
-#       If to concatenated into a single string and thus loosing ability to pass args with spaces
-# args="${*:2}"
-# shellcheck disable=SC2124
-args=${@:2}
+args=( "${@:2}" )
 
 case $command in
 
 build)
-docker compose build "$args"
+docker compose build "${args[@]}"
 ;;
 
 sh)
@@ -29,7 +23,7 @@ $RUN mermaid sh
 ;;
 
 pnpm)
-$RUN mermaid sh -c "pnpm $args"
+$RUN mermaid pnpm "${args[@]}"
 ;;
 
 dev)
@@ -41,7 +35,7 @@ $RUN --service-ports mermaid sh -c "pnpm run --filter mermaid docs:dev:docker"
 ;;
 
 cypress)
-$RUN cypress "$args"
+$RUN cypress "${args[@]}"
 ;;
 
 help|"")
```

I'll be honest, I don't really use this `run` script, but a quick test of the above changes seemed to work.

(minor)
The `SANKEY_LINK_VALUE` lines should go _after_ the `SANKEY_LINK_NODE` line to be consistent with the order in the `SankeyLink:` line.

And regarding the order of the `SANKEY_LINK_NODE` and `SANKEY_LINK_VALUE` rules, I was trying to make the point that since the `SankeyLink` rule 
```
SankeyLink:
  source=SANKEY_LINK_NODE "," target=SANKEY_LINK_NODE ","  value=SANKEY_LINK_VALUE EOL
;
```
has `SANKEY_LINK_NODE` _first_ in it and `SANKEY_LINK_VALUE` _after_  `SANKEY_LINK_NODE` , the specific rules for each of those (`..._NODE` and `...VALUE`) should _also_ appear in that order in the grammar file.  It is keeping things ordered consistently: when you see things that are in a certain order, they should appear _in that same order_ elsewhere (unless there's a reason they can't, e.g. some technical reason).  

This grammar file is so trivial that it's not a big deal. At all.  (which is why I called it "minor")
Sorry for the late reply. You're correct.
Should we delete these, if we're not using them in this diagram?
